accept: Cowork plugin manifest for act exists (likely just a thin pointer to the binary plus the MCP tool registration).
A Cowork session can be configured with the plugin and immediately use act without a manual install step.
If a prebuilt-binary-in-repo path is taken instead, justified in the issue with rationale and the install instructions are on whichever path was chosen.
payload.description: Cowork's runtime sandbox mounts directories (not arbitrary files or ~/), so an installed-once approach (brew or ~/go/bin/act) doesn't carry across Cowork sessions. Options: (a) target repo commits a prebuilt linux/amd64 binary into bin/ — fast but bloats every repo; (b) Cowork plugin manifest that pulls the release binary at session start — clean but requires the plugin to exist; (c) inline 'go install' as part of session bootstrap — works but adds ~10s per session. Per the brief, the intent was a Cowork plugin manifest pointing at the binary; that needs to actually exist. Investigate Cowork's plugin format and ship the manifest.
payload.title: make act available in Cowork sessions
